sections.heading: RT
sections.content: {"role": "Adjunct to surgery for locoregional control, palliation of symptoms, and treatment of unresectable disease.", "principles": ["EBRT is considered for gross residual disease after surgery, unresectable locoregional recurrence, and palliation of symptomatic metastases.", "IMRT technique is encouraged to reduce toxicity.", "For anaplastic carcinoma, EBRT should start as quickly as possible; for R0/R1 resection, adjuvant EBRT should begin 2–3 weeks postoperatively.", "Concurrent chemoradiation may be considered for anaplastic carcinoma.", "EBRT is not routinely recommended for differentiated thyroid carcinoma if RAI is effective and no gross residual disease."], "dose_frameworks": [{"name": "Adjuvant EBRT for Differentiated/Medullary Carcinoma (R1 resection)", "total_dose": "60–66 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8–2 Gy", "fractions": "30–33", "schedule": "Daily fractions, Monday–Friday.", "indication": "Microscopic residual disease (thyroid bed, involved lymph nodes). Elective nodal regions 50–56 Gy. [THYR-C]"}, {"name": "Salvage EBRT for Differentiated/Medullary Carcinoma (R2 resection or inoperable)", "total_dose": "66–70 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8–2 Gy", "fractions": "33–39", "schedule": "Daily fractions.", "indication": "Gross disease. Microscopic disease 60–66 Gy. [THYR-C]"}, {"name": "Adjuvant EBRT for Anaplastic Carcinoma (R0/R1)", "total_dose": "60–66 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.2 Gy twice-daily or 1.8–2 Gy once-daily", "fractions": "25–33 (twice-daily) or 30–33 (once-daily)", "schedule": "Twice-daily with 6-hour interval or once-daily.", "indication": "Microscopic disease/high-risk regions. Elective nodal regions can be treated with SIB: 45–54 Gy. [THYR-C]"}, {"name": "Salvage EBRT for Anaplastic Carcinoma (R2 or inoperable)", "total_dose": "66–70 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.2 Gy twice-daily or 1.8–2 Gy once-daily", "fractions": "28–33 (twice-daily) or 33–39 (once-daily)", "schedule": "Twice-daily or once-daily.", "indication": "Gross disease. Microscopic/high-risk regions 60–66 Gy. [THYR-C]"}, {"name": "Palliative EBRT for Metastases", "total_dose": "8 Gy in 1 fraction; 20 Gy in 5 fractions; 30 Gy in 10 fractions", "dose_per_fraction": "8 Gy, 4 Gy, or 3 Gy", "fractions": "1, 5, or 10", "schedule": "Daily or single fraction.", "indication": "Symptomatic bone or soft tissue metastases. Higher doses (45–60 Gy) for oligometastatic disease with good performance status. [THYR-C]"}], "approaches": [{"name": "Concurrent Chemoradiation for Anaplastic Carcinoma", "dose_fractionation": "EBRT as above with concurrent radiosensitizing chemotherapy (e.g., weekly paclitaxel, carboplatin/paclitaxel).", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Carboplatin/paclitaxel or weekly paclitaxel. [THYR-B]", "indication": "Unresectable or incompletely resected anaplastic carcinoma.", "key_trial": "Phase 2 studies show improved local control and survival. [539, 540]", "toxicities": "Mucositis, esophagitis, myelosuppression, dermatitis."}, {"name": "Stereotactic Radiosurgery (SRS) for Brain Metastases", "dose_fractionation": "Typically 18–24 Gy in 1 fraction for solitary lesions.", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "None.", "indication": "Solitary or limited CNS metastases from any thyroid carcinoma type.", "key_trial": "NCCN Guidelines for Central Nervous System Cancers.", "toxicities": "Radionecrosis, edema."}]}

timeline.title: Adjuvant EBRT for Differentiated/Medullary Carcinoma (R1 resection)
timeline.body: Total dose: 60–66 Gy, dose per fraction: 1.8–2 Gy, fractions: 30–33, schedule: Daily fractions, Monday–Friday. Indication: Microscopic residual disease (thyroid bed, involved lymph nodes). Elective nodal regions 50–56 Gy. [THYR-C]

timeline.title: Salvage EBRT for Differentiated/Medullary Carcinoma (R2 resection or inoperable)
timeline.body: Total dose: 66–70 Gy, dose per fraction: 1.8–2 Gy, fractions: 33–39, schedule: Daily fractions. Indication: Gross disease. Microscopic disease 60–66 Gy. [THYR-C]

timeline.title: Adjuvant EBRT for Anaplastic Carcinoma (R0/R1)
timeline.body: Total dose: 60–66 Gy, dose per fraction: 1.2 Gy twice-daily or 1.8–2 Gy once-daily, fractions: 25–33 (twice-daily) or 30–33 (once-daily), schedule: Twice-daily with 6-hour interval or once-daily. Indication: Microscopic disease/high-risk regions. Elective nodal regions can be treated with SIB: 45–54 Gy. [THYR-C]

timeline.title: Salvage EBRT for Anaplastic Carcinoma (R2 or inoperable)
timeline.body: Total dose: 66–70 Gy, dose per fraction: 1.2 Gy twice-daily or 1.8–2 Gy once-daily, fractions: 28–33 (twice-daily) or 33–39 (once-daily), schedule: Twice-daily or once-daily. Indication: Gross disease. Microscopic/high-risk regions 60–66 Gy. [THYR-C]

timeline.title: Palliative EBRT for Metastases
timeline.body: Total dose: 8 Gy in 1 fraction; 20 Gy in 5 fractions; 30 Gy in 10 fractions, dose per fraction: 8 Gy, 4 Gy, or 3 Gy, fractions: 1, 5, or 10, schedule: Daily or single fraction. Indication: Symptomatic bone or soft tissue metastases. Higher doses (45–60 Gy) for oligometastatic disease with good performance status. [THYR-C]

timeline.title: Stereotactic Radiosurgery (SRS) for Brain Metastases
timeline.body: Dose fractionation: Typically 18–24 Gy in 1 fraction for solitary lesions. Indication: Solitary or limited CNS metastases from any thyroid carcinoma type.
